steps.title: Square both sides
steps.content: Square $\sqrt{k-x}=57-x$ to clear the radical: $$k-x=(57-x)^2=3249-114x+x^2.$$ Note that any solution must also satisfy $57-x\ge 0$ so the right side is non-negative.

steps.title: Apply the unique-solution condition
steps.content: The smallest $k$ giving a valid solution is the tangency case $\Delta=0$: $$4k-227=0 \Rightarrow 4k=227.$$ The repeated root $x=\tfrac{113}{2}=56.5$ satisfies $x\le 57$, so it is valid. The answer is 227.
